intro: Stelis longispicata, also known as Long-spurred Stelis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis longispicata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Stelis longispicata is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
